Nvidia’s Jensen Huang becomes first CEO to roll out token budgets for recruitment
description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang proposed offering engineers annual "token budgets" as part of their compensation, potentially amounting to half their base salary. This move aims to boost engineer productivity and serve as a new recruiting tool in Silicon Valley. Huang believes this will amplify engineers' capabilities, making them significantly more effective.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang updates on China after CFO flags no H200 revenue
description: Nvidia is set to resume sales of its H200 processors in China after receiving purchase orders and clearance from both US and Chinese authorities. This marks a significant shift following months of uncertainty and export restrictions. The company anticipates regaining a critical market for its global data center business.

title: How AI is rewriting the rules of work for the specialist
description: Erran Berger, the Vice President of Product Engineering at LinkedIn, thinks the former type of division of labour is rapidly disappearing, a prediction that could unsettle traditionalists in the software world

title: Sam Altman sends 'thanks note' to engineers with the message that 'we plan to take your job'
description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed gratitude to early software engineers, acknowledging their complex work. However, his comments sparked debate about AI's impact on coding jobs, with users interpreting his message as a prelude to displacement. This follows recent workforce reductions and AI investment announcements from companies like Block Inc. and Atlassian, signaling a broader trend of automation affecting white-collar roles.

title: India’s frogs are finding allies from citizen science to sanctuaries
description: India is home to more than 450 amphibian species, many of them threatened by climate change and habitat loss. Conservation programs across the country are turning the tide — and ordinary citizens can now join the effort
